title: Week Ahead — Market Outlook
headline: A dense convergence of June CPI and PPI, retail sales, Fed Chair Warsh's maiden Capitol Hill testimony, Q2 bank earnings, monthly options expiry, and a re-escalating US-Iran conflict will test a market sitting near record highs beneath a deceptively calm VIX.
volatilityRationale: While spot VIX sits at a moderate ~16, the convergence of June CPI, PPI, retail sales, Fed Chair Warsh's first semi-annual testimony, Q2 bank earnings kickoff, monthly options expiry, ~$290B Treasury settlement, and a re-escalating US-Iran conflict creates a multi-catalyst event window. Beneath the calm surface, the UBS VIX spike-prediction model is at a 10-month high, the SKEW index is elevated at 145-150, and Nasdaq volatility is at its highest since 2002 relative to the S&P 500 — all signaling that markets are walking into volatility despite the low spot reading.

recap.text: The S&P 500 advanced 1.2% for the week to close at 7,575.39 on July 10, with the Nasdaq up 1.7% and the Dow down 0.5%, as indexes held near record highs despite turbulence beneath the surface.

FOMC minutes released July 8 revealed a deeply split Federal Reserve under new Chair Kevin Warsh, with a few officials advocating for an immediate rate hike and 9 of 18 projecting at least one hike by year-end; the committee removed all forward guidance and dropped its easing-bias language.

June nonfarm payrolls came in at just 57,000 versus ~115,000 expected, with 74,000 in downward revisions to April and May, cooling near-term rate-hike odds from ~67% to ~50% for September and pushing the more likely timing of any move toward December.

The United States and Iran continued trading military strikes during the week, with fresh US strikes reigniting geopolitical anxiety and threatening to derail a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending the four-month conflict; oil prices remained on track for weekly gains.

The 30-year Treasury bond auction cleared at 5.058% on July 9, the highest yield since 2007, as part of approximately $290 billion in July coupon auctions (3-year, 10-year, 30-year) set to settle on July 15.

The CBOE VIX remained in a low-volatility regime around 15.8-16.9, but the SKEW index stayed elevated at 145-150 and the UBS VIX spike-prediction model hit a 10-month high, signaling growing tail-risk hedging beneath the calm surface.

triggers.text: June CPI release (8:30 AM ET): consensus headline -0.1% MoM (prev. +0.5%), core +0.3% MoM (prev. +0.2%), with annual core rate unchanged at 2.9% YoY; sharp oil-price declines following the US-Iran MOU expected to weigh on headline. A hot print could re-accelerate rate-hike bets and test the 10Y yield above 4.70%.

Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first semi-annual testimony to the House Financial Services Committee (text release 8:30 AM ET), followed by Senate testimony on July 15; traders will scrutinize for any explicit thresholds that could trigger a rate rise, though Warsh has notoriously leaned against forward guidance.

Q2 bank earnings season kicks off: JPMorgan, BlackRock, Wells Fargo, and Citigroup report pre-market (Jul 14); Bank of America and Morgan Stanley (Jul 15); Goldman Sachs (Jul 16); Netflix after the close (Jul 16). Bank lending-growth thesis tracking above 8% YoY.
June PPI release (8:30 AM ET): consensus +0.2% MoM (prev. +1.1%), core +0.4% MoM matching May; Fed Beige Book also released. Analysts will use CPI and PPI to model June PCE expectations due July 30.
June Retail Sales (8:30 AM ET): consensus headline +0.3% MoM (prev. +0.9%), core ex-autos expected -0.1% MoM; also weekly Jobless Claims and Philly Fed Manufacturing Index.

Monthly options expiration (third Friday of July): standard equity and ETF options expire, coinciding with US Building Permits, Industrial Production, Import/Export Prices, and University of Michigan Consumer Expectations data.

watch.text: Monitor the CPI-PPI-retail sales data sequence as a 'dual-binary' print: a soft CPI (core +0.2% MoM or lower) could push S&P 500 toward 7,600, while a hot print (+0.4% MoM, +3.2% YoY) risks a break below 7,400 and a 10Y re-test of 4.60-4.70%. Options imply a ±1.5% move for the week.

Watch US-Iran escalation headlines: continued military strikes and any collapse of the ceasefire MOU could spike oil prices, reignite inflation fears, and trigger the risk-off flows already being hedged via the elevated SKEW and UBS VIX spike-prediction model.

Position for the Nasdaq volatility divergence: the Cboe NDX Volatility Index near 27 is at its highest since 2002 relative to VIX, suggesting tech and mega-cap earnings (Netflix Jul 16; big tech Jul 22-31) and AI capex guidance could be the primary volatility driver even if broad-market VIX stays subdued.

Track the approximately $290B Treasury coupon settlement on July 15 (from July 6-9 auctions) as a potential liquidity drain, and watch Warsh testimony signals on rate-hike thresholds that could move the 2-year yield and reprice September FedWatch odds currently near 50%.
